questions.prompt: What type of relationship does inheritance represent?
questions.explanation: Inheritance is an 'is-a' relationship. A MemberCashCard IS-A CashCard — it inherits all the parent's features and adds its own.

questions.prompt: What does super().__init__() do inside a child class constructor?
questions.explanation: super().__init__() calls the parent class constructor to set up the inherited instance variables before the child adds its own.

questions.prompt: A child method calls super().topUp(amount) then adds extra logic. This is overriding by:
questions.explanation: Overriding by refinement means the child calls the parent's method (via super()) and then adds extra behaviour on top.

questions.prompt: Can you create an object directly from an abstract class?
choices: No, you must create a child class first
questions.explanation: Abstract classes cannot be instantiated directly. You must create a concrete child class that implements all the abstract methods.

questions.prompt: A list contains Circle, Rectangle, and Triangle objects. You call .area() on each. This demonstrates:
questions.explanation: Polymorphism means different classes respond to the same method call (.area()) in their own way. Each shape calculates area differently.
